etymology.description: 浴(浴) (yù, "to bathe") + 室(室) (shì, "room") = A room for bathing, hence bathroom.
etymology.interpretation: 浴(浴) (yù, "to bathe") depicts immersion in water for washing or cleansing -> place for this activity. 室(室) (shì, "room") is an enclosed space -> together they create a space designated for bathing activities.

examples.chinese: 我在浴室洗了个澡(我在浴室洗了個澡)。
examples.pinyin: Wǒ zài yùshì xǐ le gè zǎo.
examples.english: I took a shower in the bathroom.

examples.chinese: 她喜欢在浴室里唱歌(她喜歡在浴室裡唱歌)。
examples.pinyin: Tā xǐhuān zài yùshì lǐ chànggē.
examples.english: She likes to sing in the bathroom.

examples.chinese: 浴室里的灯坏了(浴室裡的燈壞了)，需要修理(需要修理)。
examples.pinyin: Yùshì lǐ de dēng huài le, xūyào xiūlǐ.
examples.english: The light in the bathroom is broken and needs repair.
